ssdsims 0.0.0.9015

Ship One Shard to an Upload Destination

Source: R/upload.R
ssd_upload_shard.Rd

A generic, dispatched on the upload object's class, that ships one shard Parquet to the destination and returns the local path (so the paired upload_<step> target stays format = "file"). For an Azure destination it uploads the file at path to <url>/<container>[/<prefix>]/<step>/<partition-path>/part.parquet (the optional prefix subdirectory from ssd_upload_azure()); when the required credentials are absent it aborts with a loud error - never a silent no-op - so intent to skip the network is only ever expressed by passing ssd_upload_dryrun(). For a dry-run destination it performs no network I/O, records a skip, and returns the local path.

Usage

ssd_upload_shard(path, upload)

Arguments

path

The local shard Parquet path (the <step>_step target's value).

upload

An upload destination from ssd_upload_azure() or ssd_upload_dryrun().

Value

The local path (a string), so the upload_<step> target stays format = "file".

Adding a backend

The destination set is open and extended by a constructor-plus-methods contract - no edit to the existing methods. To add S3, GCS, or another backend:

  1. Write a constructor returning an object of class c("ssdsims_upload_<backend>", "ssdsims_upload") that validates its destination at construction (as ssd_upload_azure() validates its url and container) and carries no credentials.

  2. Implement the generic methods for that class: ssd_upload_shard() (ship one shard, return the local path), ssd_test_upload() (the credentials/connectivity probe, failing loud on a missing credential), ssd_open_uploaded() (read the uploaded results back in place), and ssd_summarise_uploaded() (the in-place fan-in summary).

The package ships only the Azure and dry-run backends; no speculative backends are added.

See also

ssd_test_upload(), ssd_open_uploaded(), ssd_scenario_targets().

Examples

path <- tempfile(fileext = ".parquet")
file.create(path)
#> [1] TRUE
ssd_upload_shard(path, ssd_upload_dryrun())
#> Dry-run upload: skipped "/tmp/RtmpNB1ZLE/file36bf313d6cef.parquet".
#> [1] "/tmp/RtmpNB1ZLE/file36bf313d6cef.parquet"

On this page
